query: Which top 3 entities had the most number of filings in FERC's rule making proceedings on large load interconnection in December 2025?
final_answer:
## Answer

The FERC rulemaking proceeding on large‑load interconnection is **Docket No. RM26‑4‑000**, "Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System" — the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANOPR) FERC opened in October 2025 at the direction of DOE Secretary Chris Wright (CSIS, csis.org; McGuireWoods; FERC eLibrary).

I searched FERC's eLibrary for all filings submitted in that docket between **December 1–31, 2025**, which returned **63 filings**, and tallied the number of filings per filing entity (counting each docket submittal once per author organization).

### Key finding
The December 2025 filing activity was almost entirely **a single round of reply comments**, with nearly every entity filing exactly **one** document. As a result, there is no meaningful "top 3" by volume — only two entities filed more than once:

| Rank | Entity | # of filings in Dec 2025 |
|------|--------|--------------------------|
| 1 (tie) | **Switch, Ltd.** | 2 (Reply Comments + a doc‑less Motion to Intervene, both 12/05/2025) |
| 1 (tie) | **PJM Interconnection, L.L.C.** | 2 (Reply Comments 12/11/2025 + Request to Update Service List 12/29/2025) |
| 3 (large tie) | ~55 other entities | 1 each |

Every other party — e.g., Google LLC, OpenAI Group PBC, Constellation Energy, ENGIE North America, the Data Center Coalition, NARUC, Edison Electric Institute, the California PUC, Virginia SCC, AEP, Southern Companies, MISO Transmission Owners, Indicated PJM Transmission Owners, Vistra, Talen Energy, and many others — submitted exactly one filing during the month.

**Source:** FERC eLibrary Advanced Search, Docket RM26‑4‑000, filed dates 12/01/2025–12/31/2025 (63 records; saved as `/work/ferc_search_20260614_053818.csv`).

### Caveats / assumptions
- I counted each distinct **docket submittal** crediting an "AUTHOR" organization. Joint filings (e.g., the Southern Environmental Law Center coalition, the Industrial Energy Consumers of America group, the Distributed Capacity Parties) name multiple co‑authors but were submitted as a single filing, so each member is credited with one filing.
- December's filings were dominated by the **reply‑comment deadline (December 5, 2025)**; initial comments were due in November 2025, so most participants filed only once in December.
- Because the result is effectively a two‑way tie at the top (Switch and PJM, 2 filings each) followed by a ~55‑way tie at one filing each, the "top 3" cannot be cleanly distinguished beyond those two entities. If you instead intended the **full comment record** (Nov–Dec 2025), the picture would differ — roughly 150 parties filed across the proceeding (computelaw.blog), but that spans more than December alone.

First, I'm trying to identify the correct docket number—it's likely something like RM25-9 or possibly AD24-11—so I can search FERC's records for the relevant filings.

steps.content:
## Answer

The FERC rulemaking proceeding on large‑load interconnection is **Docket No. RM26‑4‑000**, "Interconnection of Large Loads to the Interstate Transmission System" — the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANOPR) FERC opened in October 2025 at the direction of DOE Secretary Chris Wright (CSIS, csis.org; McGuireWoods; FERC eLibrary).

I searched FERC's eLibrary for all filings submitted in that docket between **December 1–31, 2025**, which returned **63 filings**, and tallied the number of filings per filing entity (counting each docket submittal once per author organization).

### Key finding
The December 2025 filing activity was almost entirely **a single round of reply comments**, with nearly every entity filing exactly **one** document. As a result, there is no meaningful "top 3" by volume — only two entities filed more than once:

| Rank | Entity | # of filings in Dec 2025 |
|------|--------|--------------------------|
| 1 (tie) | **Switch, Ltd.** | 2 (Reply Comments + a doc‑less Motion to Intervene, both 12/05/2025) |
| 1 (tie) | **PJM Interconnection, L.L.C.** | 2 (Reply Comments 12/11/2025 + Request to Update Service List 12/29/2025) |
| 3 (large tie) | ~55 other entities | 1 each |

Every other party — e.g., Google LLC, OpenAI Group PBC, Constellation Energy, ENGIE North America, the Data Center Coalition, NARUC, Edison Electric Institute, the California PUC, Virginia SCC, AEP, Southern Companies, MISO Transmission Owners, Indicated PJM Transmission Owners, Vistra, Talen Energy, and many others — submitted exactly one filing during the month.

**Source:** FERC eLibrary Advanced Search, Docket RM26‑4‑000, filed dates 12/01/2025–12/31/2025 (63 records; saved as `/work/ferc_search_20260614_053818.csv`).

### Caveats / assumptions
- I counted each distinct **docket submittal** crediting an "AUTHOR" organization. Joint filings (e.g., the Southern Environmental Law Center coalition, the Industrial Energy Consumers of America group, the Distributed Capacity Parties) name multiple co‑authors but were submitted as a single filing, so each member is credited with one filing.
- December's filings were dominated by the **reply‑comment deadline (December 5, 2025)**; initial comments were due in November 2025, so most participants filed only once in December.
- Because the result is effectively a two‑way tie at the top (Switch and PJM, 2 filings each) followed by a ~55‑way tie at one filing each, the "top 3" cannot be cleanly distinguished beyond those two entities. If you instead intended the **full comment record** (Nov–Dec 2025), the picture would differ — roughly 150 parties filed across the proceeding (computelaw.blog), but that spans more than December alone.
steps.reasoning: Looking at the filing counts, Switch and PJM Interconnection each submitted two filings, while the rest filed just once. Switch's two submissions were reply comments and a motion to intervene, while PJM filed reply comments and a service list update request. So the top filers are Switch and PJM tied at two filings each.
